title: BRONCHOGEN
description: Bronchogen is a bioregulator peptide developed in Russia, specifically targeting bronchial and lung tissue. It belongs to the Khavinson peptide family and is used to support respiratory function and promote lung tissue regeneration.

research.title: Effect of the peptide bronchogen (Ala-Asp-Glu-Leu) on DNA thermostability
research.summary: Study found that bronchogen increases DNA melting temperature, acting as a stabilizing agent.

research.title: Short Exogenous Peptides Regulate Expression of CLE, KNOX1, and GRF Family Genes in Nicotiana tabacum
research.summary: Short peptides including bronchogen significantly influence tobacco plant cell culture growth at very low concentrations.

research.title: Peptides tissue-specifically stimulate cell differentiation during their aging
research.summary: Certain short peptides boost differentiation marker expression in human embryonic fibroblasts, suggesting geroprotective potential.

research.title: Penetration of short fluorescence-labeled peptides into the nucleus in HeLa cells and in vitro specific interaction of the peptides with DNA
research.summary: Short biologically active peptides penetrate animal cell nuclei and interact with DNA and RNA.

research.title: Modulating Effect of Peptide Therapy on the Morphofunctional State of Bronchial Epithelium in Rats with Obstructive Lung Pathology
research.summary: One-month bronchogen therapy in rats reversed structural damage from COPD, restoring bronchial epithelium immune and barrier functions.

sentiment.summary: Limited user reports available. Those with respiratory conditions report some improvements, but evidence is primarily from Russian research.

effects.label: Limited Western Data:
effects.description: Most research comes from Russian sources.

effects.label: Subtle Effects:
effects.description: Benefits may be gradual and subtle.

effects.label: Best for Chronic Conditions:
effects.description: Most reports involve chronic respiratory issues.

dosage.label: Cycling:
dosage.description: Often used in cycles with breaks between courses.

availability.label: Russian Suppliers:
availability.description: Primarily available from Russian peptide suppliers.

availability.label: Specialized Retailers:
availability.description: Some international nootropic vendors carry it.
